text:
Admission Date:  2001-05-17              Discharge Date:   2014-05-07

Date of Birth:  2000-09-04             Sex:   F

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
No Known Allergies / Adverse Drug Reactions

Attending:Obrien
Chief Complaint:
"pneumonia"


Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
-1978-10-02 bronchoscopy with biopsy
-1998-04-21 pericardial window, chest tube placement


History of Present Illness:
Ms. Davis is a 62 year old female with a history of limited
scleroderma/CREST and recurrent pneumonia who was admitted for
suspected pneumonia.  She has been having persistent symptoms
since summer 1994 when she was diagnosed with a pneumonia and
completed a course of cefpodoxime/azithromycin.
.
During the week PTA, the patient had progressively worsening
cough, shortness of breath and fever.  Her fever curve hit
maximum at 101.9.  She wasn't able to perform her usual daily
functions at home due to profound fatigue and decided to go to
6935 Lutz Flats
Piercetown, NJ 10952-0169 for further evaluation.  On 11-26, the patient had a
chest x-ray which showed a left lower lobe pneumonia with
progression and worsening. She does report that over the past
months it has gotten progressively more difficult for her to
swallow.
.
In the ED, initial vitals 99.5 86 113/64 18 98% ra.  She had a
CXR concerning for pna and was given cefepime.  They encouraged
her to accept admission to the hospital.
.
Currently, she is resting in bed but having ongoing cough and
shortness of breath.
.
ROS:
(+) per HPI
(-) Denies headache, vision changes, rhinorrhea, congestion,
sore throat, cough, shortness of breath, chest pain, abdominal
pain, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, BRBPR, melena,
hematochezia, dysuria, hematuria.


Past Medical History:
- Scleroderma with CREST syndrome
- GERD/Barrett's esophagus
- Raynaud's disease
- Osteonecrosis of jaw from reclast
- Osteoporosis
- R rotator cuff injury s/p surgery
- R wrist injury


Social History:
Lives with her husband. Worked as a social worker and in
information services at Brownsville Doctors Hospital is now retired and stays at home.

- Tobacco history: 45 pack years, quit 2004
- ETOH: social
- Illicit drugs: denies

Family History:
No family history of early MI, arrhythmia, cardiomyopathies, or
sudden cardiac death
- Mother: had sarcoid, died at 81
- Father: had Wegener's granulomatosis, died at 84
- Aunt has scleroderma

Physical Exam:
ADMISSION PHYSICAL EXAM:
VS - Temp 98.8F, BP 114/60, HR 116, R 20, O2-sat 97% RA
GENERAL - chronically ill appearing female
HEENT - NC/AT, PERRL, EOMI, sclerae anicteric, MMM, OP with
fullness and enlargement of the left posterior pharynx
NECK - supple, no thyromegaly, no JVD, no carotid bruits
LUNGS - CTA bilat, no r/rh/wh, good air movement, resp
unlabored, no accessory muscle use
HEART - RRR, no MRG, nl S1-S2
ABDOMEN - NABS, soft/NT/ND, no masses or HSM, no
rebound/guarding
EXTREMITIES - WWP, no c/c/e, 2+ peripheral pulses (radials, DPs)
SKIN - no rashes or lesions
LYMPH - + cervical LAD which is tender
NEURO - awake, A&Ox3, CNs II-XII grossly intact, muscle strength
10-07 throughout, sensation grossly intact throughout, steady gait
.
DISCHARGE PHYSICAL EXAM:
Physical Exam:
VS: 96.7, 98/58, 78, 20, 100% on 4L
General: chronically ill appearing woman in NAD, comfortable
HEENT: NC/AT, sclerae anicteric, MMM, OP clear
Neck: supple, no thyromegaly; + left anterior cervical LAD;
Lungs: decreased BS at bases, o/w CTA
Heart: RRR, nl S1-S2, no MRG
Abdomen: +BS, soft/NT/ND, no masses or HSM
Extrem: WWP, no c/c/e


Pertinent Results:

MCV-87 MCH-28.4 MCHC-32.8 RDW-13.0 Plt Ct-506*

Eos-1.1 Baso-0.6

K-4.4 Cl-101 HCO3-26 AnGap-17
2016-03-27 05:30AM BLOOD Calcium-9.1 Phos-3.7 Mg-2.2

.
MICROBIOLOGY:
.
11-02 Sputum culture (expectorated): RESPIRATORY CULTURE (Final
1998-04-21):
      SPARSE GROWTH Commensal Respiratory Flora.
      GRAM NEGATIVE ROD(S).    SPARSE GROWTH.
      GRAM NEGATIVE ROD #2.    SPARSE GROWTH.
04-20 Pericardial fluid studies: No PMNs or microorganisms, final
fluid culture with no growth
01-26 Pericardial fluid: 4+ PMNs, no microorganisms, prelim NGTD

01-26 Pleural fluid: 4+ PMNs, no microorganisms, prelim NGTD
05-16 Blood Cx:
***
.
CYTOLOGY
-04-20 pericardial fluid: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANT CELLS.
Predominantly blood.
-01-26 pleural fluid: Pleural fluid, left side: POSITIVE FOR
MALIGNANT CELLS, consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma
-01-26 pericardial fluid: Pericardial fluid: ATYPICAL. Rare
atypical epithelioid cells. Numerous neutrophils and lymphocytes

-11-02 level 7: FNA, Level 7 lymph node: POSITIVE FOR MALIGNANT
CELLS,  consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma. Scattered
lymphocytes suggestive of lymph node sampling.
-11-02 Left lower lobe mass: FNA, Left lower lobe mass: POSITIVE
FOR MALIGNANT CELLS, consistent with adenocarcinoma.
-11-02 4R Lymph node: FNA, 4R lymph node: POSITIVE FOR MALIGNANT
CELLS,   consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma. No definite
evidence of lymph node sampling.
-11-02 11L Lymph node: FNA, 11L lymph node: POSITIVE FOR
MALIGNANT CELLS,  consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma.
Scattered lymphocytes suggestive of lymph node sampling.
2000-01-06 2:59 pm PLEURAL FLUID      PLEURAL FLUID.
   GRAM STAIN (Final 2000-01-06):
      3+  (5-10 per 1000X FIELD):   POLYMORPHONUCLEAR
LEUKOCYTES.
      NO MICROORGANISMS SEEN.
      This is a concentrated smear made by cytospin method,
please refer to
      hematology for a quantitative white blood cell count..
   FLUID CULTURE (Preliminary):    NO GROWTH.
   ANAEROBIC CULTURE (Preliminary):    NO GROWTH.
.
PATHOLOGY:
-01-26 pericardium tissue: Organizing pericarditis. There is no
evidence of malignancy in this sample.
-01-26 left pleural cell block: Positive for malignant cells,
consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma; see note. Note: By
immunohistochemistry, the tumor cells are positive for CK7,
TTF-1, Chapman-31, CEA (absorbed), and B72.3, and are negative for
LeuM1 (CD15), calretinin, and WTI, with adequate controls. The
findings are non-specific, but are compatible with a tumor of
lung origin. Correlation with clinical and radiographic findings
is recommended.
-11-02 Lymph node FNA's combined: POSITIVE FOR MALIGNANT CELLS,
consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma.
.
- pleural fluid 2000-01-06
DIAGNOSIS:
Pleural fluid, cell block:
Metastatic adenocarcinoma.
.
IMAGING:
.
- 02-11 CXR: There has been progression of left-sided multifocal
pneumonia, with dense consolidation in the left perihilar
region, superior
segment of the left lower lobe, and lateral basal segment of the
left lower lobe. Partial obscuration of the left hemidiaphragm
is noted. There are no significant pleural effusions or
pneumothorax. Marked lymphadenopathy persists in the bilateral
hilar regions, left greater than right; as well as in the
subcarinal region, with splaying of the left and right main
bronchi.  The heart is normal in size. Pleural parenchymal
scarring persists at the lung apices, particularly in the
lateral right upper lobe. Multilevel degenerative changes are
noted in the thoracic spine.
IMPRESSION: Worsening multifocal left-sided pneumonia, with
associated
intrathoracic lymphadenopathy. Recommend close clinical and
imaging followup as an underlying malignancy is not excluded.
Consider pulmonary consultation.
.
09-02 CT CHEST: IMPRESSION:
1. Since 2008-06-21, the left lower lobe consolidation has
significantly
progressed with unchanged bulk of the bilateral hilar and
mediastinal lymph
nodes. Interlobular interstitial septal thickening in left lower
lobe
persists.
2. Interval appearance of mild to moderate simple left pleural
effusion and
mild-to-moderate pericardial effusion without any element of
pericardial
tamponade.
Above CT features are worrisome for lung malignancy. Bulky
subcarinal lymph
node can be approached for transbronchial biopsy.
3. 8.3-mm hypodense nodule in the left lobe is stable since 10-19, 1994.
Suggest ultrasound of thyroid for further evaluation.
4. 7-mm hypodensity in the right lobe of liver is incompletely
characterized. Suggest USG abdomen for further evaluation.
.
1998-04-21 TTE: Right ventricular chamber size and free wall motion
are normal. There is a moderate pericardial effusion which
measures up to 2 cm anterior to the right ventricle in the
subcostal views and 1.4 cm adjacent to the inferolateral wall.
The effusion is echo dense, consistent with blood, inflammation
or other cellular elements. There is brief right atrial
diastolic collapse. There is no right ventricular collapse.
.
2003-05-07 cxr: IMPRESSION: Since 1991-12-21, following left
chest tube removal, there is a tiny residual left apical
pneumothorax. Bilateral moderate pleural effusions, lower lung
atelectasis, and mid and lower lung opacities are unchanged in
appearance.
.
MRI HEAD w and W/O CONTRAST (2003-05-07):
IMPRESSION:
1. Small, lacunar early subacute infarct in the left posterior
frontal lobe.
2. No evidence of cerebral metastasis.
.
PET SCAN 2019-04-15:
IMPRESSION: 1. In this patient with known left lung
adenocarcinoma, high FDG uptake in the left hilar mass, and the
resultant post-obstructive consolidation in the left lower lobe.
2. FDG avid mediastinal, both hilar and right supraclavicular
adenopathy 3. Midly increased uptake in the left adrenal gland,
is concerning for a metastasis. Recommended attention on follow
up imaging. 4. Interval worsening of multiple FDG avid right
lung opacities, suggestive of multi-focal pneumonia. Bilateral
moderate pleural effusions.
.
CXR 2000-01-06
IMPRESSION: Unchanged left apical tiny pneumothorax. Bilateral
pleural
effusions and associated atelectasis are stable since the
previous exam.
.

MCV-88 MCH-28.8 MCHC-32.7 RDW-14.1 Plt Ct-592*

K-4.1 Cl-91* HCO3-37* AnGap-11
2002-02-12 05:57AM BLOOD Calcium-8.5 Phos-3.9 Mg-1.7


Brief Hospital Course:
Ms Davis is a 62yoM with h/o limited scleroderma/CREST and
recurrent PNA who initally p/w PNA. After receiving a CT scan
for the PNA, she was found to have a pericardial effusion that
was evaluated by ultrasound. She received a pericardial window
for cardiac tamponade presumably from a malignant effusion, as
well as a bronchoscopy which took several transbronchial
biopsies. She was transferred to the MICU, and was eventually
called out to the cardiology floor, for management of new
paroxysmal afib with RVR while the etiology of her left lung
mass was being worked up. She was transferred to the oncology
floor after her initial pathology results showed malignant cells
c/w adenocarcinoma.
.
ACTIVE PROBLEMS BY ISSUES:
.
# Malignancy adenocarcinoma: CT showed that since 2008-06-21, her left lower lobe consolidation has significantly
progressed with unchanged bulk of the bilateral hilar and
mediastinal lymph nodes; interlobular interstitial septal
thickening in left lower lobe persists. After pathology from her
bronchoscopy (lymph node and LLL mass Bx's) were positive for
malignant cells consistent with adenocarcinoma, heme-onc was
consulted and she was transferred to the oncology floor. She
began her chemotherapy regimen which is to be continued as an
outpatient.
.
# Pericardial effusion: CT thorax showed evidence of pericardial
effusion. Pt c/o SOB, and TTE showed evidence of tamponade
physiology. She was urgently brought to the cath lab for
pericardiocentesis. There, 200cc of bloody fluid was taken out,
and a drain was put in with another 230cc drainage over the next
12 hours. Drainage then tapered off to nothing. Repeat TTE
showed evidence of returned pericardial effusion with evidence
of loculation and tamponade physiology. Pt also continued to
complain of SOB, and had tachycardia. CT surgery was consulted
and agreed that patient needed urgent pericardial window
operation.
.
Pericardial window was performed on 01-26. Likely etiology of the
effusion was malignancy in the context of LLL consolidation and
pleural effusion with likely post-obstructive PNA. After
call-out to the cardiology floor, her chest tube drainage
decreased and the tube was removed on 01-17. Repeat CXR's
continued to show stable effusions. The pt c/o constant dyspnea
and was maintained on several L of O2 with sats in the mid-90's;
she was started on low doses of morphine to treat dyspnea and
air hunger on 05-16, which gave her significant symptomatic
improvement.
.
# Pneumonia:  She likely had a post-obstructive pneumonia due to
the lung mass seen on CT chest one month ago.  A repeat CT chest
was performed and showed the mass was amenable to bronchoscopic
biopsy.  Pulmonology was consulted and performed a biopsy and
bronchoscopy with washings sent for cultures.  She was treated
with cefepime and metronidazole for 10 days each for concern for
anaerobes and resistant gram negatives. Her cough and lung exam
initially improved, but on 05-16 her dyspnea increased, she had
persisting opacities on CXR and slowly increasing WBC, and so
zosyn, and levofloxacin were started. She tolerated this regimen
and her symptoms improved. She still has intermittent dyspnea
and coughing that is well controlled with morhpine.
.
# Paroxysmal afib: New onset this admission in MICU on morning
of 11-02. Since undergoing surgery for placement of pericardial
window, pt has been intermittently in atrial fibrillation with
RVR to 130s. Denies any cardiac history or known h/o afib.
Patient was symptomatic during RVR episodes with dyspnea, with
systolic blood pressures 90-100s (baseline SBP appears to be
100s-120s). Given that she currently has a pericardial drain in
place and is going for transbronchial biopsy, anticoagulation
was deferred.

On the cardiology floor, metoprolol was continued at 12.5mg q6hr
given relatively low BPs; she self-converted into sinus with HR
in the 70-80's.  Deferred starting anticoagulation with aspirin
given CHADS2-vasc score of one in setting of recent procedures.
TSH was normal. During her stay on the cardiology floor, she
remained in sinus rhythm with HR in 70-80's. The cardiologists
decided that the isolated episodes of AFIB were likely d/t the
pericardial drain. Throughout the remainder of her stay, she had
no further episodes of AFIB. She was continued on the metoprolol
nonetheless.
.
# Normocytic Anemia: Hct was in low 30's during admission
(recent baseline 30-35), MCV 85, RDW 13. Stable from previous
admission 1 month ago. Likely anemia of chronic inflammation;
retic count, ferritin, B12/folate are all WNL. Also could be
concerned for hemolysis as she has known autoimmune disease
(CREST), but tbili normal and high LDH. Deferred iron
replacement for now given acute illness.
.
# Lymphadenopathy: She had tender cervical lymphadenopathy and
also posterior pharynx adenopathy.  This is likely related to
the mass in her lung and biopsies are pending.
.
# Lacunar stroke: The patient was incidentally found to have a
small lacular infarct on MRI. She exhibited no symptoms.  It was
thought that the hypercoagulable state along with the brief AFIB
may have been the cause. Nonetheless, neurology was made aware,
and they recommended a carotid series and aspirin initiation.
The patient is also to have an echo done as an outpatient.
.
CHRONIC PROBLEMS BY ISSUE:
.
# Limited scleroderma with CREST syndrome: Her symptoms are
moderately controlled with Calcium channel blockers at home.
She had some symptoms of worsening esophageal dysmotility due to
this.  She was continued on nifedipine and her proton pump
inhibitor for esophageal involvement.
.
# Osteonecrosis of the jaw: History of osteonecrosis secondary
to zolandronate.
She was continued on her calcium and vitamin D for prevention as
well as ibuprofen for pain control, taken especially with meals.
 Also, she was continued on her chlorhexidine mouth washes.
.
TRANSITIONAL ISSUES:

Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
St. Elizabeth Medical Center Nursing & Therapy Center - USNV Jones
FPO AE 68840 (47896 Gross Brook
Elizabethfurt, RI 58158-8748 Center
for Rehabilitation and Sub-Acute Care)

Discharge Diagnosis:
PRIMARY DIAGNOSES:
Post-obstructive pneumonia
Adenocarcinoma

SECONDARY DIAGNOSIS:
limited scleroderma


Discharge Condition:
Mental Status: Clear and coherent.
Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive.
Activity Status: Out of Bed with assistance to chair or
wheelchair.


Discharge Instructions:
Dear Ms. Davis,
.
You were admitted to the hospital because you were having
fevers, fatigue, and persistent cough after your last treatment
for pneumonia.  We think the reason that your pneumonia
continued is because you had a mass in your lung which was
making it difficult for your lungs to clear out the infected
mucus.
.
A bronchoscopy was performed to evaluate this mass, and the
results of the biopsy showed adenocarcinoma.  You were given
antibiotics for treatment of the pneumonia, and you were
transferred to the oncology floor for further management of your
cancer. While there, you were started on your chemotherapy
regimen. A pleurex a placed for palliative purposes. Your
chemotherapeutic regimen was terminated.
.
The following changes were made to your medications:

- STOP taking levofloxacin
- STOP taking penicillin
- STOP taking nifedipine
- START metoprolol
- START albuterol and ipratropium
- START fluconazole which you will take for 2 more days

Followup Instructions:
PSC 7288, Box 0490
APO AP 70223 St. Elizabeth Medical Center Building - HEM ONC
3:30 PM on 1987-11-03
Dr. Bell
